description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with irregular, naturalistic edges and a weathered surface. The block displays a mottled coloration, primarily gray-green with patches of brown and hints of moss or lichen, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. Its surface is pitted and uneven, with visible textural variations and some minor indentations, giving it a rugged, aged appearance. One side features a more pronounced concave area, possibly from erosion or chipping, while the other faces are flatter but still irregular. The block appears dense and solid, typical of quarried stone used in urban landscaping or as a barrier. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial modifications beyond natural wear.
description: A weathered, irregularly shaped stone block with a mottled gray-green and brown surface, featuring rough texture and natural pitting.
description_view_0: The block is seen from its broadest, flattest face, showing a mostly gray-green surface with some brown discoloration and visible pitting.
description_view_1: This view highlights a more vertical face with a pronounced concave indentation and a mix of greenish and brown hues.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is visible, showing a flatter, more uniform gray-green surface with subtle textural variations.
description_view_3: A narrower end of the block is shown, emphasizing the irregular, rough edges and a mostly greenish-gray coloration.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with pitting, roughness, and some moss or lichen growth, especially on the edges. There are no major chips but the block appears aged and exposed to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or loosely aligned with other blocks
